pi_items.source: Task specifies 150 BPM minimum. Clinical tachycardia is defined as >100 BPM (American Heart Association). The 150 BPM threshold is above clinical threshold but appropriate for demonstration purposes to show pronounced alarm state.
Task specifies 300 BPM as maximum. This is a reasonable upper limit for simulated vital signs testing. Clinical significance: severe tachycardia but within simulation range.
Confirmed in task.json setup_task.sh which removes this file before task execution and export_result.sh which checks for this file after task completion.
Confirmed in task.json setup_task.sh which removes this file before task execution and export_result.sh which checks for this file after task completion.

Confirmed in OpenICE environment. Cross-referenced with create_simulated_device@1 and view_device_vitals@1 tasks in the same environment which explicitly reference 'Multiparameter Monitor' as the Device Type for simulated devices in OpenICE.

Confirmed in OpenICE Supervisor demo applications. Verified through clinical_app_tour@1 task in the same environment which explicitly lists 'Vital Signs' as one of four available clinical demonstration applications in OpenICE Supervisor.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses synthetic data from OpenICE medical device simulator. Target heart rate minimum is 150 BPM (above clinical tachycardia threshold of >100 BPM but appropriate for demonstration of alarm systems). Maximum is 300 BPM. The required device 'Multiparameter Monitor' and application 'Vital Signs' are standard OpenICE simulator components confirmed in other tasks within this environment. All file paths and metadata values have been verified against task scripts and related environment tasks.
